conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: For the object storage, let's use AWS S3. It's reliable and has great integration with our other services.

conversation.speaker: assistant
conversation.text: S3 is the industry standard. I'll set up the buckets and lifecycle policies.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: But S3 is expensive for data transfer. Let's use Cloudflare R2 instead. It has no egress fees.

conversation.speaker: assistant
conversation.text: R2 is S3-compatible and cheaper. I'll migrate the upload endpoints.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: We need immutable versioning. Google Cloud Storage has better versioning support. Let's use GCS.

conversation.speaker: assistant
conversation.text: GCS is solid. I'll set up the object versioning.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: We want to keep our infrastructure simple. Let's just use S3. The cost difference is negligible for our scale.

query: What object storage service is being used for file uploads?
